sections.heading: How triggers work
sections.body:
A trigger sits inside a subquest. When the game evaluates the scenario (every tick), it checks each active trigger. If all of the trigger's conditions are satisfied, its actions fire.

By default, a trigger fires **once** and is then disabled. Set `isRepeating: true` for continuous triggers (e.g. weekly resource grants).

sections.heading: AND vs OR logic
sections.body:
Each trigger has a `conditionsLogic` setting: **AND** (all conditions must be true) or **OR** (any one condition must be true).

For complex logic ‚Äî e.g. '(A and B) or C' ‚Äî use multiple triggers with separate condition sets.

sections.heading: Event-based conditions
sections.body:
These conditions fire when a specific event occurs:

- `SquadKill` ‚Äî a neutral squad is defeated
- `HeroKill` ‚Äî a specific hero is defeated
- `SquadInteraction` ‚Äî player approaches a neutral squad
- `ObjectInteractionBefore` / `ObjectInteractionAfter` ‚Äî player interacts with a map object
- `ObjectLose` ‚Äî player loses a capturable object
- `ObjectCaptureEntity` ‚Äî player captures an object
- `NodeRevealed` ‚Äî fog of war is lifted at a node
- `PlayerDefeated` ‚Äî a player is eliminated

sections.heading: State-based conditions
sections.body:
These conditions check the current game state:

- `Counter` ‚Äî checks a counter's value: `quest_stage >= 2`
- `UnitOwnSide` / `UnitOwnHero` ‚Äî checks unit counts in armies
- `ResCounter` ‚Äî checks resource stockpile
- `ItemOwnSide` ‚Äî checks artifact ownership
- `DifficultyCustomMap` ‚Äî checks map difficulty setting

sections.heading: Time-based conditions
sections.body:
These conditions fire at specific points in time:

- `StartTurn` ‚Äî fires at the start of a specific turn for a specific player (or any turn if omitted)
- `StartWeek` ‚Äî fires at the start of any week (every 7 turns)
- `AnyStartTurn` ‚Äî fires at the start of any player's turn

sections.heading: The counter extra field
sections.body:
Some conditions (`UnitKill`, `UnitLose`, `UnitHire`, `StartTurn`) have an extra **Counter** field. This sets a threshold: the condition only fires after it has been met N times.

For example: `UnitKill` with `counter: 10` fires only after 10 of that unit type have been killed.
sections.note: This counter field is separate from the counter SID system ‚Äî it's a raw number, not a reference to a named counter.

sections.heading: No conditions
sections.body: A trigger with no conditions fires immediately when the subquest becomes active. Use this for 'on quest start' actions like showing an introduction dialog or placing quest markers.
sections.warning: A no-condition trigger combined with `isRepeating: true` fires every tick. This is almost never what you want.
